source: US Census (July 2025); NABOR June 2026 YoY data (trailing 30 days); Walmart/Tyson/J.B. Hunt corporate announcements; relocation-buyer research. Compiled in Second Brain knowledge/pages/nwa-market-2026.md. Refresh flag: regional data ages by late 2027 — re-run NABOR trends and census check if reused.

region.growth_rank: 9th-fastest-growing US metro; first top-10 entry since at least 2020
anchors: Walmart new 350-acre Bentonville HQ campus building/coming online
region.demand_concentration: Central Benton County / NE Washington County

pricing_barbell.implication: Pricing strategy must be segment-specific; mass-market positioning misses barbell dynamics.

overpricing_penalty.statement: Listings that require a price reduction after debuting more than 10% over eventual sale-comparable value spend roughly 5x longer on market than correctly priced listings.

relocation_buyers.budget_profile: Larger budgets than local market average; unfamiliar with NWA neighborhoods
relocation_buyers.positioning_opportunity: Relocation buyers fit consultative/advisory positioning: neighborhood education, market context, local knowledge. Trust and local knowledge rank high in seller decisions.

outlook.appreciation_context: NWA historically outperforms national appreciation (~5%+ vs 2-3% national)
outlook.data_gaps: No hard data on NWA agent/brokerage market saturation or market share; competitive landscape is qualitative signal only (one national-affiliate brokerage, one self-described largest independent, many small teams)

marketing_roi.note: No hard NWA-specific photo/floor-plan ROI figures were present in the sourced market brief (nwa-market-2026.md). Using widely-cited, non-NWA-specific industry benchmarks as directional placeholders pending a sourced NWA figure; mark as such in generated reports and do not present as NABOR data.
marketing_roi.source: industry-general benchmark, not NABOR/NWA-sourced -- replace when a sourced figure is available
